companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: BREAKTHROUGH on Day 15: The Trump administration granted limited Mythos 5 access to roughly 100 US companies and federal agencies on June 26, with Commerce Secretary Lutnick signing a letter confirming 'appropriate safeguards' for trusted partners. Fable 5 remains offline — the administration did not address Fable 5 in yesterday's directive, but talks continue this weekend with an eye toward Fable 5 restoration. Biometric ID verification (Persona) effective July 8 (11 days). AI for Science event with John Jumper is June 30 (3 days). October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target intact; $65B Series H at $965B valuation closed May 28.

items.headline: Mythos 5 restored for 100+ US companies/agencies — Day 15; Fable 5 still offline, talks continue this weekend
items.detail: The US government granted Anthropic permission on June 26 to release Mythos 5 to approximately 100 companies and federal agencies. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick wrote in an official letter: 'appropriate safeguards are in place to permit certain trusted partners to access the Claude Mythos 5 Model.' The directive did NOT address Fable 5. Axios reported June 27 (today) that the Trump administration is now close to allowing Anthropic to restore Fable 5 access as well — insiders expect the limits on Fable 5 could be lifted as soon as this coming week. The Pentagon and NSA still need to give Fable 5 the green light; Anthropic expects conversations with the government to continue over the weekend. The ban began after a Senate hearing on June 11 where General Joshua Rudd reported Mythos broke into classified systems 'in hours, not weeks,' citing a narrow jailbreak.

items.headline: AI for Science event June 30 with Nobel laureate John Jumper — 3 days away
items.detail: Nobel laureate John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize winner in Chemistry and AlphaFold2 co-creator — joined Anthropic from Google DeepMind (announced June 19). His first public Anthropic appearance is confirmed for 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event on June 30 at 10 AM PST — now 3 days away — featuring life sciences executives and research demonstrations.

items.headline: Biometric ID verification effective July 8 (11 days); October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; $65B Series H
items.detail: Starting July 8, 2026 — now 11 days away — Anthropic can require government-issued photo ID, live selfie, and facial geometry scan from consumer Claude (Free, Pro, Max) users via third-party vendor Persona Identities. Business/API plans are exempt. The biometric pathway is viewed as a potential mechanism for US-verified access to Fable 5 if the ban lifts. Anthropic's confidential S-1 filed June 1 targets an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1T first-day market cap. Revenue run rate exceeds $30B. The $65B Series H closed May 28 at a $965B valuation.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: Russell 3000 reconstitution took effect at close of June 26 — institutional passive-fund rebalancing in CoreWeave and IREN begins at Monday's open, June 29 (2 days away). NVIDIA Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) are due June 30 (3 days). NVDA faces a broad AI/tech selloff triggered partly by OpenAI IPO delay reports and OpenAI-Broadcom 'Jalapeno' chip news that could challenge NVIDIA's dominance. Vera Rubin is in full production; NVIDIA runs 81% of the TOP500 and 90% of new systems.

items.headline: Russell 3000 institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29 — CoreWeave and IREN inclusion active
items.detail: The Russell 3000 reconstitution took effect after US market close on June 26. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing buying in CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN Limited begins at Monday's open, June 29 — 2 days away. NVIDIA holds approximately a $2 billion (~11%) stake in CoreWeave (purchased at $87.20/share in January 2026) and a $2.1 billion investment option in IREN. The dual inclusions trigger mandatory passive rebalancing across hundreds of index-tracking funds, increasing institutional visibility for NVIDIA's infrastructure investment network. CRWV traded near $100.88 ahead of inclusion.

items.headline: Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) due June 30 — 3 days; OpenAI-Broadcom 'Jalapeno' chip a new competitive threat
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting on June 24; proxy voting results will be filed via Form 8-K within four business days, by June 30. Seven proposals were on the ballot: director elections, PwC ratification, executive compensation, and four shareholder proposals. Separately, OpenAI and Broadcom are co-developing a custom AI chip called 'Jalapeno' on TSMC 3nm, which analysts say could reduce NVIDIA GPU dependency for inference workloads. NVIDIA runs 81% of the TOP500 and 90% of new systems as of June 23. Vera Rubin is in full production for hyperscale AI factory deployment. A broad AI/tech selloff is weighing on NVDA amid IPO timing uncertainty.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash has been released as generally available (GA) — the first in the Gemini 3.5 series. Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed to July 2026. Gemini 3.5 Flash outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo), and MCP Atlas (83.6%), and leads in multimodal understanding on CharXiv (84.2%). Noam Shazeer departure to OpenAI confirmed. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think remains the current flagship at 82.4% GPQA Diamond.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash released GA — outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic and coding benchmarks
items.detail: Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash as the generally available (GA) model, now powering gemini-flash-latest. Gemini 3.5 Flash outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on key benchmarks: Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo), MCP Atlas (83.6%), and leads on CharXiv (84.2%). Google describes it as 'the strongest agentic and coding model yet, excelling at complex long-horizon tasks.' Note: this is Gemini 3.5 Flash, not Gemini 3.5 Pro, which remains delayed to July. Older gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and gemini-3-pro-image-preview models were deprecated June 25.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro delayed to July; Noam Shazeer confirmed departed to OpenAI; Gemini 2.5 Pro leads at 82.4% GPQA Diamond
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — expected to feature a 2M token context window, Deep Think reasoning, and pricing of ~$15 per million input tokens — remains officially delayed to July 2026, the first publicly missed model window from Google DeepMind this year. Noam Shazeer (VP Engineering, Gemini co-lead, 'Attention Is All You Need' co-author) has confirmed his departure for OpenAI — the fourth senior researcher to leave in 2026. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think (82.4% GPQA Diamond) remains the current flagship for AI Ultra subscribers ($250/month).

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 4 days away. Maia 200 has launched as Microsoft's most advanced in-house AI inference processor, built on TSMC 3nm with 100B+ transistors, 10+ petaflops at FP4, and 30% better performance-per-dollar than NVIDIA hardware. It is live at a data center in Iowa with a second location planned in Arizona. Anthropic Maia 200 chip negotiations ongoing — Anthropic would be first external customer. Copilot Chat now allows Claude as a model selection. Microsoft 365 Copilot specialization renamed July 2026.

items.headline: Maia 200 AI chip launched — TSMC 3nm, 10+ petaflops FP4, 30% better performance/dollar than NVIDIA; Iowa data center live
items.detail: Microsoft has brought its Maia 200 AI accelerator online at a data center in Iowa, with a second location planned in Arizona. The chip has 100B+ transistors, delivers over 10 petaflops at 4-bit precision and ~5 petaflops at 8-bit, built on TSMC 3nm. Microsoft says it achieves 30% better performance-per-dollar than existing NVIDIA infrastructure for inference workloads. Maia 200 is the silicon at the center of ongoing negotiations with Anthropic — if finalized, Anthropic would be Maia 200's first external customer. MSFT stock popped ~2% on the Maia 200 news.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork grace period ends July 1 (4 days); Copilot Chat adds Claude model selection; specialization renamed July
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Frontier preview tenants must configure usage-based billing (Copilot Credits, $0.01/Credit) by July 1 or lose access — now 4 days away. Copilot Chat now allows users to select Anthropic's Claude as an AI model alongside OpenAI models. Microsoft 365 Copilot specialization is being renamed starting July 2026. CSP partners can offer 15% off net partner price for Copilot to customers committing to 3-year/300+ license agreements through September 30, 2026.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) launched June 26 in limited preview for ~20 government-approved partner companies — not yet broadly available. GPT-4.5 was removed from ChatGPT on June 26. Codex Remote is GA on all plans. OpenAI IPO tracking to 2027; Bloomberg confirmed the delay June 26. 42-state AG probe ongoing. ChatGPT serves 900M+ weekly active users but market share is below 50%. The Jalapeno chip co-developed with Broadcom in 9 months is in development. OpenAI and Broadcom chip news triggered a broad AI stock selloff.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna launched June 26 — limited preview for ~20 government-approved companies; broader July rollout
items.detail: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 on June 26 in a limited, government-coordinated preview for approximately 20 partner companies approved by the US government. Three model tiers: Sol (flagship, $5/$30 per 1M tokens), Terra (balanced, $2.50/$15), Luna (fast/affordable, $1/$6). GPT-5.6 includes stronger coding and reasoning vs. GPT-5.5. General availability expected in coming weeks (July). GPT-4.5 was simultaneously removed from ChatGPT including custom GPTs. Codex Remote — allowing users to start/continue work on a connected Mac or Windows host from the ChatGPT mobile app — reached GA on all plans.

items.headline: OpenAI-Broadcom 'Jalapeno' chip co-developed in 9 months — could challenge NVIDIA AI chip dominance
items.detail: OpenAI's custom AI accelerator chip 'Jalapeno' was co-developed with Broadcom in just nine months, reflecting deep software-hardware co-development. Jalapeno is designed for AI inference workloads and, alongside Broadcom's custom ASIC expertise, represents one of the most significant moves by a frontier AI lab to reduce NVIDIA GPU dependency. The news contributed to a broad AI chip stock selloff.

items.headline: 2027 IPO confirmed by Bloomberg; 900M+ weekly users; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT below 50% market share
items.detail: Bloomberg confirmed June 26 that OpenAI is leaning toward a 2027 IPO, citing operational readiness and market timing concerns. OpenAI confidentially filed an S-1 with the SEC June 8. ChatGPT now serves 900M+ weekly active users with ~20% of queries expressing direct commercial intent. Despite scale, ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% (Sensor Tower) — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%. The 42-state AG probe (led by NY AG Letitia James, opened June 12) remains active.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta is developing an AI-powered prediction market app (codename 'Antwerp' / 'FBForecast') using Llama to auto-generate questions and resolve markets — separate from Facebook and Instagram. Meta Glasses (launched June 23) are shipping. AI Mode on Facebook is live. 2026 capex guidance of $115–135B unchanged. AMD MI450 samples shipping under the $60B/6GW partnership.

items.headline: Meta building AI prediction market app using Llama — separate from Facebook/Instagram
items.detail: Meta plans to release an AI-powered prediction market app (codenamed 'Antwerp' / 'FBForecast') where users can wager on real-world events using play money. Llama will auto-generate questions from trending topics and make personalized market recommendations. Meta's AI will also resolve markets. The app will be separate from Facebook and Instagram. NPR reported the development on June 24.

items.headline: Meta Glasses shipping at $299; Cannes Lions AI ad tools; 2026 capex $115–135B; AMD MI450 samples arriving
items.detail: Meta Glasses (Fury, Adventurer at $299; Starfire at $399) with Muse Spark AI shipped June 23. At Cannes Lions 2026, Meta announced end-to-end AI creative solutions for marketers and unified creator partnership tools turning authentic content into high-performing ads. 2026 capex guidance of $115–135B unchanged. AMD MI450 samples are shipping under the Meta-AMD $60B/6GW partnership; CoreWeave $21B agreement through December 2032 proceeding.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS launched Fully Managed Knowledge Bases on Bedrock with Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever at AWS Summit New York 2026. Web Search on Bedrock AgentCore is GA in US East. OpenAI GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex are now GA on Bedrock. Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1/2/3 compliant. Grok 4.3 remains on Bedrock. Claude Fable 5's prior availability on Bedrock was disrupted by the government ban — Mythos 5's partial restoration may affect Bedrock availability.

items.headline: AWS Summit NY: Bedrock Fully Managed Knowledge Bases GA; Web Search on AgentCore GA in US East
items.detail: At AWS Summit New York 2026 (late June), Amazon launched Bedrock Fully Managed Knowledge Bases — simplifying enterprise RAG pipelines with native data connectors, Smart Parsing for automatic multi-format data preparation, and an Agentic Retriever for complex multi-step queries, all integrated with AgentCore Gateway. Web Search on Bedrock AgentCore — a fully managed tool enabling agents to ground responses in current, cited web knowledge with zero data egress — is now generally available in the US East (N. Virginia) Region.

items.headline: OpenAI GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex now GA on Bedrock; Grok 4.3 and Claude remain on roster
items.detail: AWS announced general availability of OpenAI models GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex on Amazon Bedrock, broadening the multi-model roster. Grok 4.3 (1M token context, tool calling, structured output, response streaming) remains on Bedrock. The Cerebras-AWS partnership adds CS-3 silicon tier for ultra-fast decode alongside AWS Trainium 3 for prefill. Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant with failure, intent, and trajectory insights GA.

items.headline: Amazon announces $13B additional India AI/cloud investment — total $48B committed by 2030
items.detail: Amazon announced on June 26 plans to invest an additional $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India, bringing its total India commitment to $48 billion between 2026 and 2030. The investment covers new AWS data center regions, Trainium chip deployment, and local AI development programs across India's major tech hubs. The announcement follows AWS's $50 billion Stargate investment and reflects Amazon's strategy of expanding AI infrastructure globally across multiple geographies simultaneously.

companies.name: xAI / SpaceX
companies.summary: Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 4 days away. SPCX is trading near $153–155, recovering from the $147.11 low on June 23. The $60B Cursor (Anysphere) acquisition is progressing toward Q3 close — Cursor has 50,000+ enterprise clients (~2/3 of Fortune 500 with developers using it), $4B+ ARR, and generates 150M lines of enterprise code daily. Colossus committed revenues from outside clients now exceed $80B through 2029. SpaceX also joined the Russell 1000 at the June 26 reconstitution.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (4 days); Colossus outside revenues exceed $80B through 2029
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI begins July 1 — 4 days away. Reflection pays $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access at Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Reflection is working with the DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI. Colossus committed revenues from all outside clients (including Anthropic, Google, and Cursor) now exceed $80 billion through 2029. Both Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 are fully committed to third-party customers.
items.sourceTitle: The Next Web / MLQ News / CNBC

items.headline: SpaceX joined Russell 1000 at June 26 reconstitution; $60B Cursor acquisition toward Q3 close
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) joined the Russell 1000 as part of the June 26 FTSE Russell reconstitution — a significant index inclusion alongside CoreWeave. SPCX is recovering near $153–155 from its $147.11 low on June 23 (roughly 32% below the June 16 ATH of $225.64 set after IPO). The $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor) — announced June 16, the largest acquisition of a VC-backed startup ever — is progressing toward Q3 2026 regulatory close. Cursor has 50,000+ enterprise clients, ~$4B ARR ($2.6B from enterprise B2B), and 150M lines of daily enterprise code.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Quarterly dividend $0.65/share payable June 30 — 3 days away, marking 15 consecutive years of increases. Q2 AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance ~$16B (+200%+ YoY). Apollo and Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational. OpenAI Jalapeno chip co-developed with Broadcom in 9 months — Broadcom is also fabricating OpenAI's Titan chip on TSMC 3nm. AI chip collaboration with OpenAI positions Broadcom against NVIDIA.

items.headline: Quarterly dividend $0.65/share payable June 30 (3 days); Q2 AI revenue $10.8B +143%; Q3 guidance $16B
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 15 consecutive years of increases — is payable June 30 to holders of record June 22. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY); total quarterly revenue hit a record $22.19 billion (+48% YoY) with free cash flow of $10.26 billion (46% margin). Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion, representing more than 200% YoY growth. FY2027 target exceeds $100B across six core custom-silicon customers.

items.headline: OpenAI 'Jalapeno' chip co-developed with Broadcom in 9 months — alongside Titan on TSMC 3nm for Stargate
items.detail: OpenAI's custom 'Jalapeno' AI accelerator was co-developed with Broadcom in just 9 months through deep software-hardware co-development. Jalapeno is designed for inference workloads and represents one of the most significant moves to challenge NVIDIA GPU dependency. Broadcom is also fabricating OpenAI's first 'Titan' AI chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Combined, these two OpenAI chips cement Broadcom as the dominant custom ASIC partner for frontier AI labs. The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35B AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR bounced ~5.35% on June 26 to close near $112.40, breaking a seven-day losing streak. Michael Burry closed half of his short position — the primary catalyst. A new partnership with Zeta Global was announced. PLTR's 52-week low was $106.38 during the selloff. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline benefits from yesterday's partial Mythos 5 restoration for ~100 US agencies/companies. Down ~40% YTD.

items.headline: PLTR bounced 5.35% to ~$112.40 on June 26 — Michael Burry closes half his short; Zeta Global partnership
items.detail: Palantir (PLTR) surged approximately 5.35% on June 26, closing near $112.40 (range $107.88–$114.08), breaking a seven-day losing streak. The primary catalyst was Michael Burry disclosing he closed out half his short position in the stock, which had heavily weighed on investor sentiment. A new partnership with Zeta Global was also announced, aiming to enhance growth potential. The 52-week low was $106.38 during the selloff. PLTR remains down ~40% YTD.

items.headline: Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline: Mythos 5 partially restored for US agencies — defense pipeline impact
items.detail: The Trump administration's June 26 partial restoration of Mythos 5 access for ~100 US companies and federal agencies is the first positive development for the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline since the June 12 ban. Palantir's Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation. The Pentagon had reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of AI workflows off Anthropic. Fable 5 talks continue this weekend; a broader restoration would further benefit the pipeline. 2026 revenue guidance: $7.18–$7.20B (+61% YoY).

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 beta features expanding with practical AI tools: AI-assisted bill splitting, smart password replacement, intelligent message suggestions, calendar creation from natural language, smarter Shortcuts, contextual customer service call info, smart home grouped notifications, and AI-organized Safari tabs. The rebuilt Siri AI runs on a custom Google Gemini model (~$1B/year). OpenAI retained outside counsel over ChatGPT placement — no lawsuit filed. iOS 27 public beta planned for July; GA this fall.

items.headline: iOS 27 beta AI features expanding — bill splitting, smart passwords, Safari tabs, Shortcuts, Siri in Dynamic Island
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta testing is revealing practical AI features coming to iPhones: AI-assisted restaurant bill splitting, automatic password replacement after security breaches, intelligent message suggestions, calendar creation from natural language, smarter Shortcuts automation, contextual information during customer service calls, grouped smart home notifications, and AI-organized Safari tabs. Siri AI — running on a custom ~1.2 trillion parameter Google Gemini model (~$1B/year license) — gets a new visual experience tied to the Dynamic Island and a dedicated Siri app. iOS 27 public beta arrives July; GA this fall.

items.headline: OpenAI outside counsel over Siri placement — no lawsuit filed; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional provider
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 27. Apple is separately evaluating Mistral AI as an additional provider option for iOS 27 AI Extensions. The iOS 27 Extensions framework currently lists Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Mistral as eligible Siri AI extensions — disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act clarity.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD is recovering at ~$532–544 after the June 23 SK Hynix HBM4 selloff. Analysts confirm MI450 timeline is intact. MI450 features up to 432 GB HBM4, 20 TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 — competing directly with NVIDIA Vera Rubin. Oracle Cloud will host the first public MI450 supercluster (50,000 units in Q3 2026). AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22-23 at Moscone Center. Q2 results August 4. AMD-OpenAI 6GW strategic partnership in place.

items.headline: MI450: 432 GB HBM4, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4 — Oracle Cloud hosting first public MI450 supercluster Q3
items.detail: AMD's MI450 accelerator series features up to 432 GB of HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 performance — competing directly with NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) will host the first publicly available AI supercluster running AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs, starting with 50,000 units in Q3 2026. Meta has agreed to deploy up to 6 gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs (first wave built on custom MI450), with first gigawatt shipments scheduled for H2 2026. MI450 samples continue shipping to OpenAI and Meta.

items.headline: AMD recovering at ~$532–544; H2 MI450 timeline intact despite HBM4 selloff; Advancing AI July 22-23
items.detail: AMD shares are recovering to approximately $532–544 after the 6.3% decline on June 23 triggered by SK Hynix slowing HBM4 expansion. Analysts at Goldman Sachs (Buy), Baird ($625 target), and Barclays (Street-high $665) confirmed the selloff was a supply-chain margin concern, not a demand signal — AMD's H2 2026 MI450 production timeline remains on track. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22-23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco, expected to cover formal MI450 launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and MI500 series preview. Q2 results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, now running on Claude Opus 4.6 by default. Comet Enterprise available via MDM. Comet Plus pays publishers 80% revenue share across visits, citations, and agent actions. Perplexity raised $200M at $20B valuation in June 2026. CEO confirms 2028 IPO; Apple acquisition talks confirmed dead. ARR $500M; 100M+ MAU.

items.headline: Comet browser global on Mac/iOS — Opus 4.6 default; Enterprise MDM; 80/20 publisher revenue split
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, running on Claude Opus 4.6 as the default model (users may select others including Sonnet 4.5). Enterprise admins can deploy Comet via MDM on macOS and Windows. Comet Plus pays publishers 80% revenue across visits, citations, and agent actions — an industry-first agent-economy payout model for lead-gen publishers. Perplexity raised $200M at $20B valuation in June 2026; total raised ~$1.72B. $750M Azure infrastructure deal in place.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: Russell 3000 inclusion effective at close of June 26 — institutional passive-fund rebalancing begins at Monday's open, June 29 (2 days away). CRWV was near $100.88 at inclusion. SpaceX also joined Russell 1000 at the same reconstitution. Backlog approaching ~$131B; 2026 capex floor $31B for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. $3.1B HPC loan secured. Nine of top 10 AI model providers are customers.

items.headline: Russell 3000 effective June 26 — rebalancing begins Monday June 29; SpaceX joins Russell 1000 same day
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) entered the Russell 3000 effective after market close June 26. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing buying begins at Monday's open, June 29 — 2 days away. CRWV was near $100.88 at inclusion. SpaceX (SPCX) also joined the Russell 1000 at the same FTSE Russell reconstitution, creating a paired narrative of new AI-infrastructure companies entering major US equity benchmarks simultaneously. IREN Limited also joined the Russell 3000.

items.headline: Backlog approaching $131B; capex floor $31B; $3.1B HPC loan; 9 of top 10 AI providers as customers
items.detail: CoreWeave's contracted backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion — up ~$32B since Q1 2026 — driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. The 2026 capex floor is $31 billion. A $3.1 billion HPC loan was recently secured. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. The Nasdaq-100 inclusion took effect June 22 (closed at $117.03, +9.67%).

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Paris data center at Bruyères-le-Châtel (13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, 276 exaflops FP4) is still within its end-of-June opening window — 4 days remaining. No official opening announcement yet as of June 27. NVIDIA-Mistral Compute sovereign AI cloud launched at VivaTech June 20. $830M debt facility from seven-bank consortium. iOS 27 AI Extensions framework makes Mistral Siri-eligible. CEO Mensch leveraging Fable ban for European sovereignty narrative.

items.headline: Paris Bruyères-le-Châtel data center: 4 days remaining in end-of-June window — no official opening yet
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, ~276 exaflops FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion) is still within its end-of-June 2026 opening window — 4 days remaining as of June 27. No official opening announcement has been made. The facility is the first node in Mistral's 200 MW European compute roadmap (extending to 1.4 GW before 2030) and the flagship of Mistral Compute. A secondary Les Ulis 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026.

items.headline: NVIDIA-Mistral Compute sovereign cloud live; $830M debt facility; Mistral as Fable-ban sovereignty alternative
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European governments and enterprises — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20), built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. The $830M debt facility from seven French/international banks (led by Bpifrance, including BNP Paribas, Credit Agricole CIB, HSBC, La Banque Postale, MUFG, Natixis) is the largest AI-focused borrowing by a European tech company. CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging the Fable/Mythos ban narrative: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control.' Mistral targets EUR 1B in 2026 revenue.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Cybercab at 102+ units covering Austin (full metro), Dallas, Houston, and SF Bay Area. Service has faced criticism for long waits and occasional stalled rides approaching its one-year anniversary. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body production targets summer 2026; first external commercial (B2B) customers targeted for late 2026. Terafab in-house AI5 chip fab announced March 21. TSLA trading near flat at ~$375.

items.headline: Cybercab 102+ units; full Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, SF Bay; service criticism for wait times and stalled rides
items.detail: Tesla's Cybercab has 102+ units in the outbound lot. The unsupervised Robotaxi Service covers the full Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. As the service approaches its one-year anniversary, early results have been mixed — Insurance Journal (June 11) reported long wait times, limited availability, and cars occasionally picking up or dropping off at incorrect locations. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with the Cybercab ramp. EPA specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted).

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 mass production ongoing; first external B2B customers targeted late 2026; Terafab AI5 chip fab
items.detail: Tesla Optimus Gen 3 entered mass production at Fremont January 21, 2026. Current output is approximately 300–500 total units, all in learning/data-collection phase. V3 full-body production is targeted for summer 2026. Tesla has not opened pre-orders; first external commercial (enterprise/B2B) deployments are targeted for late 2026. Giga Texas North Campus expansion (5.2M sq ft) targets 10M robots/year. Tesla's Terafab initiative (announced March 21) is an in-house semiconductor fab for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3. FSD v14.3 launched April with 20% reduced inference latency.

Broadcom is also co-developing the OpenAI ‘Jalapeno’ custom AI accelerator (built in 9 months, on TSMC 3nm) and fabricating OpenAI’s ‘Titan’ chip for Stargate deployment.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Broadcom $0.65 quarterly dividend payable June 30 (3 days). Jalapeno chip co-development cements Broadcom’s position as the leading custom ASIC partner for frontier AI labs.

latestArticle.title: Broadcom, Apollo, and Blackstone Establish Landmark Strategic Platform to Accelerate 20GW+ of Global AI Deployments
